secondary.evidence: Summer camp June through August, that is a different animal, mixed ages, more enrichment.

value.conditions: Tuition is intentionally NOT published; shared only after a tour

value.askedQuestion: What is tuition / how much does it cost?
value.deflectionAnswer: I'd love to show you the space first — tuition depends on the program and days. Let's get you in for a tour; the number out of context loses families who would have loved us.

deposit.value: One month tuition, due at enrollment, non-refundable

visibilityPolicy.value: Do not lead with price; invite to tour first; position tuition as program/days dependent

value.label: Utilization
value.value: ~90% (52/58); room for a few more Twos

value.typical: Enrollment targets the September school-year start; tours run year-round (busiest winter–spring, slow in summer)
value.minimum: [NEEDS CONFIRMATION] not stated in the corpus

value.demographic: Working parents in Newburgh and the greater Hudson Valley with children ages 2–5; many connected to the Greater Newburgh JCC community. Word-of-mouth and JCC referrals are the dominant discovery path.
value.psychographic: Want a warm, small, personal early-learning setting over a large polished chain — they choose "the warm small one, not the slick one." Value a real relationship with the director and teachers, trust, and a not-a-factory feel; will drive past bigger/cheaper options for it.

value: small and warm
personal connection with teachers and director
clearly not a factory
JCC community connection for families who value it
reasonsChooseUs.evidence: Small, warm, they meet me and the teachers and it is clearly not a factory. And the J community, for some families that matters a lot.

keyDifferentiator.value: The warm small one, not the slick one
keyDifferentiator.evidence: We are the warm small one, not the slick one.

outcomeDelivered.value: Students reach educational, social, and emotional goals at their own pace while developing autonomy, character, and the habits of good citizenship.
outcomeDelivered.evidence: Years 1, 2 and 3 are designed to help students reach multiple tiers of educational, social and emotional goals at a pace befitting each individual learner… encourages autonomy, character development and helps raise good citizens for our future.

value.name: [SYNTHETIC — none captured; live recon ran website+GBP only; out of adapter scope — ]

comparison.value: Goddard is polished and more expensive; church preschools and New Paltz Montessori are alternatives; this school wins on warmth and human scale
comparison.evidence: There is a Goddard School out toward the highway, very polished, more expensive. A couple of church preschools. A Montessori in New Paltz some people drive to. We are the warm small one, not the slick one.

recommendedStatement.value: [operator gap-fill, synthetic e2e] Little Friends Learning Loft is the warm, small, JCC-rooted preschool in Newburgh for ages 2–5 — parents meet the director and lead teachers and leave a tour feeling calmer, not sold to. (Recommended positioning statement; operator analysis, confirm with owner.)

value.definition: A frazzled parent should feel calmer after reading us, not sold to
value.example: I hear you, the first couple weeks can be tender, and that is so normal.

value: Never make a parent feel like they are talking to a robot about their kid
antiAiRules.evidence: Never make a parent feel like they are talking to a robot about their kid.

value: a pediatrician newsletter, very plain and kind (unnamed)
admiredVoices.evidence: There is a pediatrician newsletter I get, very plain and kind. I like that. I could not name it though.

sampleCommunications.evidence: I would say something like, I hear you, the first couple weeks can be tender, and that is so normal. Tova has shepherded a hundred shy twos and she is wonderful at it. Why do not you come spend twenty minutes in the room with us before the start date. We will figure it out together.

value: automated warm reply in Rebecca's voice
offer tour times
answer basic FAQs
flag for human on anything beyond basics
steps.evidence: A warm reply right away, in my voice, that offers tour times and answers the basic questions, and then tells me a real human needs to do anything past that.

value: Child age 2–5 (NOT infants — licensed 2 and up; infants are the do-not-promise line)

definition.value: Family applies and puts down a deposit after the tour

dropOff.value: Director follow-up silence after tour — not prospect rejection
dropOff.evidence: The losses are almost always me going quiet, not them saying no.

value.scenario: After a tour (the current leak — owner goes quiet ~2 weeks and families enroll elsewhere)
steps: Same-day warm thank-you in the owner’s voice
Day 14: final warm follow-up before marking the family dormant

conversionRates.evidence: Most of them, honestly, when I actually follow up. If I had to guess, more than half.

revenuePerCustomer.value: ≈ $1,180/month full-time Pre-K (≈ $14,160/yr) before the JCC 10% discount; Threes slightly less; Twos varies by days. Roughly $12k–$14k/yr per enrolled child depending on program and discounts.

dataQualityNotes.value: No formal tracking; all inquiries managed through personal inbox
dataQualityNotes.evidence: I really do not track it, it is my inbox.

seasonality.evidence: Winter, maybe ten to twelve. Summer, two?

storage.evidence: Teachers shoot on their phones, post to a Slack channel called photos. Hundreds by now. The Instagram archive is another pile.

rights.value: Paper consent form per child's file; working spreadsheet months out of date; 6 families on no-photo list this year

value: permission spreadsheet months out of date
no-photo families not reliably identifiable without pulling physical files
gaps.evidence: a spreadsheet that is months out of date. Six families this year are no-photo. I could not tell you offhand which six without the files.

rights.value: Almost no video — a few shaky phone clips; no formal video usage rights established. Owner wants a short welcome video. Apply the same paper-permission gate used for photos to any future video.

value.cadence: [SYNTHETIC — live recon ran website+GBP only; out of adapter scope — website written-content inventory not scraped]

value.quote: We had heard how wonderful Little Friends Learning Loft was, but seeing it in action blew us away. As parents, you want your child to find a place that fosters curiosity but still maintains a sense of control, teaches compassion and connectedness, but still encourages individuality. What they've built here is a special kind of magic, and we're just thrilled that our son can be part of it. He truly loves being there!
value.theme: Parent satisfaction, child happiness, balance of curiosity and structure

testimonials.evidence: "We had heard how wonderful Little Friends Learning Loft was…" -Renata Kero, Cold Spring

reviewPlatforms.evidence: "5.0" displayed next to business name on the listing; review count not visible in this view

regulatedData.evidence: Every child needs current immunizations on file, with the religious or medical exemptions documented properly. Every staff needs fifteen hours of training every two years across specific topics, plus MAT certification for the ones who give medication, plus CPR and first aid.

dataResidency.value: United States. Client is a US (Newburgh, NY) program under NYS OCFS; all client data, the Claude workspace, and storage are kept in US regions. [operator gap-fill, synthetic e2e — provisioning default; confirm at setup]

reviewResponsePolicy.value: Human-in-the-loop for anything sensitive: child wellbeing/incidents, billing/money, upset parents, and OCFS/state matters are answered by the director (never auto-sent), matching the corpus escalation routing. primaryOutcome.value: Return to classroom 3 days/week with confidence the office runs without constant oversight
primaryOutcome.evidence: Be back in a classroom three days a week and trust that the office is not on fire. That is it. That is the whole dream.
